post.content: I had a machine coding round with uber where I got a sliding window frequency counter based question, which I solved using a queue. There were some minor issues like null pointers etc, but I fixed all of them, ran the code and executed with sample input. \nI got a mail after saying that the feedback was negative and they will not be going ahead with further rounds.\nAny idea what could have gone wrong? recruiter is not open to share feedback either.

I had an even worse experience. Not with Uber, another company I don't want to name. 

I got a "take home" assignment which I had 12 hours to solve after downloading. It was writing an entire project, so definitely not a trivial task. I solved it in about 5 hours and just to be sure I spent a few more hours testing it, I wrote more than 500 lines of unit test code. I sent in the assignment through a github repo, next day I get a rejection email with no explanation.

I would expect at least an explanation of what the problem was, since I've spent a lot of hours on this. I could've spent that time with my family and friends.

Next time someone wants to do a take home assignment I'll just say no.
i can get u .same happened with me but with different company!! Mostly happen when interviewer are in bad mood
I was about to respond to you here, but then I wrote a small article on this. Might help a broader audience\nhttps://www.linkedin.com/pulse/what-how-machine-coding-interview-rounds-banty-kumar.
